title: wollastonia serrulata
intro: Wollastonia serrulata, also known as the "pineapple weed" or "pineapple bush," is a flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, native to Australia and New Zealand. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Wollastonia serrulata include relief from digestive issues, reduction of fever, and support for immune function. Herbal preparations of Wollastonia serrulata can be found in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
